title: CLIP-style Symmetric Contrastive Loss
description:
Implement the symmetric cross-modal contrastive loss used in CLIP.

Given N image embeddings `I` and N text embeddings `T` (both L2-normalized):

1. Compute similarity matrix `S = I @ T.T / temperature`  (shape N x N)
2. Diagonal entries are the positive pairs
3. Image-to-text loss: cross-entropy on rows with diagonal as positive
4. Text-to-image loss: cross-entropy on columns with diagonal as positive

`L = (L_I2T + L_T2I) / 2`
constraints: Both I and T are L2-normalized, shape (N, D)
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def clip_loss(image_emb: np.ndarray, text_emb: np.ndarray, temperature: float = 0.07) -> float:
    """Symmetric CLIP contrastive loss.

    Args:
        image_emb: L2-normalized image embeddings, shape (N, D)
        text_emb: L2-normalized text embeddings, shape (N, D)
        temperature: Logit scale temperature

    Returns:
        Symmetric contrastive loss
    """
    pass
solution_code:
import numpy as np

def clip_loss(image_emb: np.ndarray, text_emb: np.ndarray, temperature: float = 0.07) -> float:
    image_emb = np.asarray(image_emb, dtype=float)
    text_emb = np.asarray(text_emb, dtype=float)
    N = len(image_emb)
    logits = image_emb @ text_emb.T / temperature  # (N, N)
    labels = np.arange(N)
    def cross_entropy_rows(logits, labels):
        # stable softmax cross-entropy per row
        m = logits.max(axis=1, keepdims=True)
        log_sum_exp = np.log(np.exp(logits - m).sum(axis=1)) + m[:, 0]
        return float(np.mean(log_sum_exp - logits[np.arange(N), labels]))
    l_i2t = cross_entropy_rows(logits, labels)
    l_t2i = cross_entropy_rows(logits.T, labels)
    return (l_i2t + l_t2i) / 2.0
explanation: CLIP trains a vision encoder and a text encoder jointly using symmetric contrastive loss. Each image should match its corresponding text and mismatch all others. The symmetric formulation ensures both encoders are trained equally. Low temperature (e.g., 0.07) sharpens the distribution, requiring precise matching. This loss scales efficiently: one forward pass per batch yields N^2 negative pairs.

test_cases.name: Identical embeddings low loss
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(0)
z = np.random.randn(4, 8)
z = z / np.linalg.norm(z, axis=1, keepdims=True)
loss = clip_loss(z, z.copy())
assert loss < 0.5

test_cases.name: Random embeddings positive loss
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(2)
i = np.random.randn(4, 8)
t = np.random.randn(4, 8)
i /= np.linalg.norm(i, axis=1, keepdims=True)
t /= np.linalg.norm(t, axis=1, keepdims=True)
assert clip_loss(i, t) > 0

test_cases.name: Symmetric: swapping inputs same loss
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(4)
i = np.random.randn(4, 8)
t = np.random.randn(4, 8)
i /= np.linalg.norm(i, axis=1, keepdims=True)
t /= np.linalg.norm(t, axis=1, keepdims=True)
assert abs(clip_loss(i, t) - clip_loss(t, i)) < 1e-9

test_cases.name: Loss is non-negative
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(6)
i = np.random.randn(8, 16)
t = np.random.randn(8, 16)
i /= np.linalg.norm(i, axis=1, keepdims=True)
t /= np.linalg.norm(t, axis=1, keepdims=True)
assert clip_loss(i, t) >= 0

hints: T2I: cross-entropy on columns = cross-entropy on logits.T rows
